human_verification.notes: Reviewed Issue #997 requirements for tax-operations-specific workflow handling, evidence and confidentiality expectations, advisory and signoff boundaries, scenario completeness, and validated packaging consistency.

review_notes: All required abilities across shared, execution-processing-and-fulfillment, and professional-and-information-services sets are declared and validated.

Scenario coverage includes normal tax workflow execution, incomplete inputs, missing e-file authorization, notice authority gaps, confidentiality conflicts, rework exceptions, completion-evidence blockers, adjacent-lane routing, conflicting policies, realistic terminology, and tenant adaptation.
Package remains specification-based and documents a separate promotion path for any future retained-implementation runtime.

specialist_owned_evidence: Outputs are bounded to evidence-backed tax workflow status, filing dependencies, notice routing, exception handling, and completion evidence rather than tax advice, legal interpretation, representation, or final signoff.
Source set includes Treasury Circular 230, AICPA tax standards, IRS e-file, authorization, power-of-attorney, taxpayer-data, and disclosure-use references with explicit refresh and decay posture.
Boundary logic explicitly prevents unsupported filing positions, unauthorized disclosure, unreviewed notice responses, and generic treatment of materially different tax jurisdictions or return types.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; non-blocking ambiguity is recorded as tenant and jurisdiction-specific prerequisites for specialized tax forms, authorities, and submission privileges.
Runtime remains specification-based; retained-implementation promotion requires separate governance approval, telemetry evidence, retrieval wiring, and non-regression validation.
